SyncRoot.schema_description: Log state transition PLANNING → EXECUTION to ledger and console.
Create adapter instance with: execution_plan_path, repo_root, run_id, config. This adapter bridges ACMS orchestration to MINI_PIPE execution engine.
Determine integration mode: python_api (direct import) or cli (subprocess). Decision based on: adapter_type config, MINI_PIPE module availability, performance.
Call: orchestrator.execute_plan(plan_path, variables). Register new MINI_PIPE run with ACMS run_id linkage for traceability.
Load plan from JSON, validate against Plan schema, perform variable substitution, store in orchestrator.plan attribute.
Create run record in SQLite DB: run_id, project_id, phase_id, state=pending, created_at, metadata.
Update DB: state=running, started_at=now(). Emit event: run_started to event bus.
Create EventBus instance with db_path, subscribe to run lifecycle events, enable event-driven integrations.
Call: scheduler.build_dag(plan.steps). Scheduler will construct task dependency graph.
For each StepDef: create node (step.id), create edges (depends_on → step.id). Validate: all dependencies valid, no circular dependencies, DAG connected.
Ready: steps with no unsatisfied dependencies (all depends_on succeeded). Blocked: steps with at least one dependency not yet succeeded.
Enforce plan.globals.max_concurrency (default: 1). If N steps ready and M running: can start max(0, max_concurrency - M).
For each ready step (up to concurrency limit): executor.execute_step(run_id, step_def, plan).
Sequential processing of ready steps. Each step executed (potentially in parallel via subprocess). Results collected as steps complete.
Call: router.select_tool(step_def, plan). Router analyzes: step_def.command, task metadata, router_config.json rules.
Decision tree: command=ai → AI tool, command=test → test runner, command=lint → linter, else use command as-is.

SyncRoot.schema_name: Call tools to run selected tool
SyncRoot.schema_description: Call: tools.execute_tool(tool_id, step_def, context). Optionally uses process_spawner for subprocess management, resilient_executor for retry/circuit breaker.
Substitute template variables: ${REPO_ROOT}, ${FILE_PATH}, ${TASK_KIND}. Example: "ai codegen --file ${FILE_PATH}" → "ai codegen --file src/utils.py".
Spawn subprocess with: cwd, shell, env (merged from plan.globals.env + step.env), timeout, stdout/stderr captured to PIPE.
SyncRoot.e2e_description: Parse tool output into normalized result

SyncRoot.schema_description: Wait for process completion (or timeout). If timeout: kill process, exit_code=-1, error="Timeout after Ns". If completed: read stdout/stderr, get exit_code.
For AI tools: parse JSON from stdout. For test tools: parse JUnit XML or test results. For linters: parse JSON output. Store in ToolResult.output_data.
ToolResult: {success: exit_code==0, exit_code, stdout, stderr, output_data, execution_time}.

If pattern_id in task metadata: guardrails.pre_execution_checks(pattern_id, task_data). Validate: pattern exists/enabled, paths in scope, tools allowed, no forbidden ops. Fail task immediately if critical violations.
SyncRoot.e2e_description: Update task state in orchestrator state

SyncRoot.schema_name: Record per-task results in run state
SyncRoot.schema_description: Create step_attempt record: step_attempt_id, run_id, sequence, tool_id, started_at, state=running. After completion: update state (succeeded/failed/canceled), ended_at, exit_code, output_patch_id, error_log.
SyncRoot.e2e_description: Record per-task results in run state

SyncRoot.schema_description: If pattern_id in task metadata: guardrails.post_execution_checks(pattern_id, task_result). Validate changes within max_changes limits. Detect hallucinated success: status=success BUT exit_code!=0 OR tests_passed<tests_run OR expected files missing. If hallucination: increment run_stats.hallucination_count, escalate if >=3.
Event: {event_type: step_completed, step_attempt_id, status: succeeded/failed, ...}. Stored in event bus for monitoring/auditing.
Mark completed step as SUCCESS or FAILED in DAG. Update step state in database. Emit state transition events.

SyncRoot.schema_name: Ask scheduler for next ready batch
SyncRoot.schema_description: Call: scheduler.get_ready_steps(current_state). Scheduler recomputes: steps with all dependencies satisfied, blocked steps, runnable steps (up to max_concurrency).

Apply failure policy from step_def.on_failure: - abort: Cancel all pending if step.critical==true - skip_dependents: Mark dependent steps as SKIPPED - continue: Allow independent steps to proceed Retry logic: if attempt < step_def.retries, reset to PENDING and retry.

While has_pending_or_running_steps(): - Update running steps (check completion, timeouts) - Find runnable steps (dependencies met) - Start runnable steps (up to max_concurrency) - Sleep 0.5s (polling interval) Exit when: all steps in terminal state (SUCCESS/FAILED/SKIPPED/CANCELED).

SyncRoot.schema_name: OPTIONAL: Convert AI outputs to patches
SyncRoot.schema_description: If patch_converter enabled: read AI tool stdout (unified diff format), parse into Patch objects, validate syntax and context, return normalized Patch.

If patch_ledger enabled: store patch with state=created, validate (→validated), queue (→queued), apply (→applied), verify (→verified), commit/quarantine (→committed|quarantined). Ledger: JSON append-only log.
SyncRoot.e2e_description: If remaining, loop to next batch selection

SyncRoot.schema_description: If any FAILED steps → run status=failed. If all SUCCESS or SKIPPED → run status=succeeded. If unexpected state → run status=quarantined.
Update DB: state (succeeded/failed/quarantined), ended_at, exit_code (0/1). Emit event: run_completed.
Commit database transaction. All step_attempts saved. All events flushed to event bus. Run record finalized.
Query: orchestrator.get_run_status(run_id). Wait for run state: succeeded | failed | quarantined.
Query: orchestrator.get_run_steps(run_id), orchestrator.get_run_events(run_id). Summarize: total tasks, tasks succeeded, failed, skipped, execution duration, files modified.
ExecutionResult: {success, run_id, tasks_completed, tasks_failed, execution_time_seconds, error, output_data}.
run_stats["patches_applied"] = <count from execution results>. Used to detect planning loops (planning without execution).

For each task in execution results: - Extract gap_ids from task metadata - Look up gaps in gap_registry - Determine if gap addressed: task succeeded + verified → gap resolved, task failed → gap in_progress, task skipped → gap deferred.

For each gap: if addressed → gap.status=RESOLVED, if attempted but failed → gap.status=IN_PROGRESS, if not attempted → gap.status=DEFERRED. Associate gap with: run_id, workstream_id, task_id (in metadata).
Save gap_registry.json with updated gap records. Atomic write (temp file + rename).
Call: anti_pattern_detector.detect_all(run_context). Check: hallucinated success (final summary), planning loop, all runbook patterns. Log all detections to ledger and console.

Components: run metadata, state transitions, gap statistics (discovered/resolved/in_progress/deferred), workstream statistics, guardrails statistics (violations, anti-patterns), configuration, artifacts.
File: .acms_runs/<RUN_ID>/run_status.json. Format: Comprehensive JSON with all run data.

File: .acms_runs/<RUN_ID>/summary_report.md (Markdown). Contains: executive summary, gap discovery results, planning results, execution results, guardrails summary, recommendations, links to artifacts.
Log state transition EXECUTION → SUMMARY to ledger and console.
SyncRoot.e2e_description: acms_controller logs state transition to SUMMARY

SyncRoot.schema_description: Formatted table with: Run ID, Phases completed, Gaps (discovered/resolved), Workstreams, Tasks (succeeded/failed), Guardrails (anti-patterns detected).
Options: create git branch, commit changes, leave in working tree, patch ledger. Controlled by config.commit_strategy.
Log state transition SUMMARY → DONE to ledger and console.
SyncRoot.e2e_description: acms_controller logs state transition to DONE

SyncRoot.schema_description: Final entry: {event: run_finalized, status: success|failed}. Ledger closed (no more writes).
Exit code 0: run succeeded (all critical tasks passed). Exit code 1: run failed (critical tasks failed or ACMS errors).
Format: [ACMS] Run completed successfully, Run ID, Gaps resolved, Tasks completed, Reports path.
If configured: remove intermediate build artifacts, compress logs, archive old runs (per run_retention_days config).
